entry.title: Azure Load Balancer
entry.description: Azure Load Balancer distributes incoming network traffic across multiple Azure resources for high availability and reliability.

freeTierDetails.summary: 750 hours and 15 GB data processing per month for Standard Load Balancer.
caveats: Free tier applies to Standard SKU only
Additional usage billed per hour and per GB
billingRiskNotes: Overages billed automatically if limits are exceeded

useCases: Enable high availability for internal services
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Create a new Standard Load Balancer resource.
Configure frontend IP and backend pool with your VMs.
Set up health probes and load balancing rules.
Review and create the load balancer.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to distribute incoming TCP/UDP traffic across multiple Azure resources for high availability.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require Layer 7 (application-level) load balancing or advanced routing; consider Azure Application Gateway instead.
entry.body: Azure Load Balancer provides network-level traffic distribution for high availability and scalability, with a free tier suitable for basic workloads.

entry.title: Azure Load Testing
entry.description: Azure Load Testing enables scalable cloud-based load testing for web apps and APIs with integrated Azure monitoring.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 50 virtual user hours per month.
caveats: Overages billed per additional virtual user hour
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if you exceed the free tier limits

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and create a Load Testing resource.
Configure a test plan by uploading a JMeter script or using the built-in test designer.
Set test parameters such as virtual users and duration.
Run the load test and monitor real-time metrics.
Review test results and Azure resource metrics for performance insights.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to simulate user load on Azure-hosted or public endpoints to validate performance and scalability.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited free testing, need on-premises test execution, or have strict cost controls without hard caps.
avoidIf: you have zero budget for overages
entry.body: Azure Load Testing provides a managed, scalable solution for simulating load on web apps and APIs, integrating with Azure monitoring for actionable insights. The free tier is suitable for moderate, regular testing needs, but overages may incur costs. Ideal for teams needing to validate performance and scalability of Azure-hosted services or public endpoints.

entry.title: Codecov
entry.description: Codecov provides code coverage reporting and analysis for automated testing workflows across multiple CI/CD platforms.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public repositories and limited usage on private repositories.

useCases: Monitor code coverage in open source projects
Integrate coverage checks in CI pipelines
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Codecov account using GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket.
Connect your repository to Codecov.
Add Codecov upload step to your CI configuration (e.g., GitHub Actions, Travis CI).
Run your test suite to generate coverage reports.
View coverage reports and insights on the Codecov dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Codecov to automate code coverage reporting for public repositories or small teams with limited private repos.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced analytics or have large teams with many private repositories, as these require paid plans.
entry.body: Codecov is a popular code coverage tool that integrates with major CI/CD platforms, offering free coverage reporting for public repositories and limited private usage. It helps teams and open source projects monitor and improve test coverage with automated reports and visualizations.

entry.title: Cypress
entry.description: Cypress is an open-source end-to-end testing framework for web applications with a free tier for local and cloud test runs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes unlimited local test runs and limited cloud parallelization.
limits: 500 test results per month in Cypress Cloud
caveats: Cloud features are limited on free tier
No flake detection or advanced analytics

quickstartSteps: Install Cypress via npm: npm install cypress --save-dev
Open Cypress: npx cypress open
Write your first test in the generated integration folder
Run tests locally or connect to Cypress Cloud for cloud runs
entry.whenToUse: Use Cypress for fast, reliable end-to-end testing of modern web applications, especially when you want easy local setup and CI integration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need to test non-browser environments, require advanced cloud analytics, or need unlimited cloud parallelization.
entry.body: Cypress offers a robust free tier for local and limited cloud-based web application testing, making it ideal for individuals and small teams starting with automated testing.

entry.title: DeepScan
entry.description: DeepScan is a static code analysis tool for JavaScript and TypeScript that detects code issues and quality problems.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public repositories and small private projects.
limits: Up to 200,000 lines per private repository
caveats: No advanced team features on free tier

useCases: Detecting bugs and code smells in JavaScript/TypeScript projects
Automating code review for open source repositories
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a DeepScan account using your GitHub login.
Authorize DeepScan to access your repositories.
Select the repositories you want to analyze.
Review the analysis results and fix detected issues.
entry.whenToUse: Use DeepScan to automatically find bugs and maintain code quality in JavaScript or TypeScript projects, especially for public or small private repos.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need analysis for large private repositories or require advanced team collaboration features, as these need a paid plan.
entry.body: DeepScan provides automated static code analysis for JavaScript and TypeScript, helping developers identify bugs, code smells, and maintain code quality with minimal setup.

entry.title: DevTest Labs
entry.description: Azure DevTest Labs enables fast, cost-controlled provisioning of development and test environments in the cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: No additional charge for DevTest Labs; pay only for underlying Azure resources used.
limits: No charge for lab management features
Pay for VMs, storage, and networking resources consumed
Free tier applies to eligible Azure resources only
caveats: Resource usage beyond free Azure quotas incurs standard charges
No separate DevTest Labs-specific free credits
billingRiskNotes: Costs depend on provisioned resources; monitor usage to avoid unexpected charges

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Search for 'DevTest Labs' and create a new lab.
Configure lab policies, such as VM sizes and quotas.
Add users and set up reusable VM images or formulas.
Provision VMs or environments as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use DevTest Labs when you need to quickly provision, manage, and control costs for dev/test environments on Azure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require persistent production environments or need advanced automation beyond what DevTest Labs supports.
entry.body: Azure DevTest Labs provides an easy way to create and manage cost-effective development and test environments, with built-in policies and automation for resource control.

entry.title: Goreportcard.com
entry.description: Goreportcard.com analyzes Go repositories for code quality and style issues using static analysis tools.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free unlimited analysis of public Go repositories.
caveats: Analysis may be queued during high load

useCases: Check Go code quality before merging pull requests
Automate code health checks for open source projects
Share code quality badges in project READMEs
quickstartSteps: Visit https://goreportcard.com/
Enter your public Go repository URL (GitHub, GitLab, etc.)
Click 'Generate Report' to start analysis
Review the generated report for issues and recommendations
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want a quick, automated code quality check for a public Go repository without setup or authentication.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need analysis for private repositories, non-Go projects, or require integration with CI/CD pipelines.
entry.body: Goreportcard.com provides free, instant static analysis for public Go repositories, helping developers identify code quality and style issues with no setup required.

entry.title: Kogiqa
entry.description: Kogiqa offers free AI-powered QA test generation and management for web apps, with integrations for popular frameworks.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes AI test generation, test management, and limited test runs per month.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Kogiqa account.
Connect your web app repository or project.
Configure basic project settings and select integrations.
Use AI to generate initial test cases.
Run tests and review results in the dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Kogiqa when you need quick, automated QA test generation and management for web applications with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited test runs, advanced customization, or support for non-web platforms.
entry.body: Kogiqa provides AI-powered QA automation for web apps, enabling fast test generation, management, and execution with a generous free tier for small projects.

entry.title: Loader.io
entry.description: Loader.io offers simple cloud-based load testing for web applications and APIs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows basic load testing with limited concurrent clients and tests.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Loader.io account.
Add and verify your target domain.
Create a new test specifying the number of clients and duration.
Run the test and review the results dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Loader.io to quickly test how your web app or API handles concurrent traffic up to 10,000 clients.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced analytics, multi-host testing, or require more than 10,000 concurrent clients per test.
entry.body: Loader.io provides a straightforward way to test your web application's or API's ability to handle concurrent users, making it ideal for quick performance checks and early-stage scalability validation.

entry.title: LoadMill.com
entry.description: LoadMill provides automated API and load testing tools for web applications with a free tier for small teams.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited monthly test runs and users for API and load testing.

useCases: Basic load testing for web services
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free LoadMill account.
Create a new project and define your API endpoints.
Configure test scenarios and parameters.
Run your tests and review the results in the dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use LoadMill when you need simple automated API or load testing for small projects or early-stage development.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced integrations, high concurrency, or extensive team collaboration beyond the free tier limits.
entry.body: LoadMill.com offers a free tier for automated API and load testing, suitable for small teams and early-stage projects with basic requirements.

entry.title: OTHRio Test Cloud
entry.description: OTHRio Test Cloud provides managed cloud environments for automated software testing and CI/CD workflows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited test environment hours and basic CI/CD integration.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an OTHRio account.
Create a new test project in the dashboard.
Connect your code repository (e.g., GitHub, GitLab).
Configure test environment settings.
Trigger your first test run.
entry.whenToUse: Use OTHRio Test Cloud to automate software testing or integrate cloud-based test environments into your CI/CD workflows.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need high concurrency, advanced environment customization, or intend to run production workloads.
entry.body: OTHRio Test Cloud offers managed cloud environments for automated testing, suitable for integrating with CI/CD pipelines and supporting basic test automation needs.

entry.title: Percy.io
entry.description: Percy.io provides automated visual testing for web applications to catch UI changes before they reach production.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 5,000 screenshots per month for public and private projects.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Percy.io account.
Install the Percy SDK for your framework (e.g., percy-cli, @percy/cli).
Integrate Percy into your test suite or CI pipeline.
Run your tests to capture and upload screenshots.
Review visual diffs and approve changes in the Percy dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Percy.io when you need to automate visual regression testing for web apps and catch unintended UI changes during development.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Percy.io if your project exceeds 5,000 screenshots/month or requires on-premise hosting, as higher usage needs paid plans.
entry.body: Percy.io enables teams to automate visual regression testing, integrating with CI/CD workflows to catch and review UI changes before deployment.

entry.title: Qase.io
entry.description: Qase.io is a cloud-based test management platform for organizing, executing, and tracking software tests collaboratively.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for up to 3 users with unlimited test cases and projects.

useCases: Centralizing manual and automated test cases
Collaborative test planning for small teams
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Qase.io account.
Create your first project from the dashboard.
Add test cases manually or import from supported formats.
Organize test suites and assign them to team members.
Execute test runs and review results in the dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Qase.io when you need a simple, collaborative test management tool for small teams or early-stage projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced permissions, custom domains, or need to support more than 3 users on the free plan.
entry.body: Qase.io offers a free, user-friendly test management platform ideal for small teams to organize, execute, and track software testing collaboratively.

entry.title: Scan.coverity.com
entry.description: Scan.coverity.com offers free static code analysis for open source projects to detect security and quality defects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free static code analysis for qualifying open source projects.
caveats: Not available for private or commercial codebases
Manual approval process may delay access
billingRiskNotes: No billing or overage risk for approved open source projects

useCases: Detecting security vulnerabilities in open source code
Improving code quality for public repositories
Integrating static analysis into CI pipelines
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Scan.coverity.com account.
Register your open source project and provide repository details.
Wait for project approval by the Coverity team.
Download and configure the Coverity analysis tool.
Run analysis and upload results to Scan.coverity.com.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need free, automated static analysis for open source projects to find security and quality issues.
entry.whenNotToUse: Do not use for private, commercial, or closed-source projects, or if you need immediate onboarding without approval delays.
avoidIf: you need analysis for private code
your project is not open source
entry.body: Scan.coverity.com provides free static code analysis for open source projects, helping maintainers find and fix security and quality issues before release.

entry.title: SeoTest.me
entry.description: SeoTest.me offers free, instant SEO analysis tools for websites and web pages.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free SEO analysis with no registration required.

useCases: Validate basic SEO for new pages
quickstartSteps: Go to https://seotest.me/
Enter your website URL in the search box.
Click 'Test SEO' to start the analysis.
Review the results and suggested improvements.
entry.whenToUse: Use SeoTest.me for fast, free SEO checks on new or existing web pages without registration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced SEO analytics, historical data, or API integration for automated workflows.
entry.body: SeoTest.me provides a simple, free way to analyze website SEO and identify on-page issues instantly, making it ideal for quick checks and learning.

entry.title: TestingBot.com
entry.description: TestingBot provides cross-browser and mobile app testing automation in the cloud for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan offers limited parallel tests and minutes for browser and mobile automation.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free TestingBot account.
Create a new project and obtain your API key and secret.
Install Selenium or Appium in your project.
Configure your test scripts to use TestingBot credentials.
Run your first automated test on the TestingBot cloud.
entry.whenToUse: Use TestingBot when you need quick, cloud-based browser or mobile test automation for web or mobile apps.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited parallel tests, advanced debugging, or private device access without upgrading.
entry.body: TestingBot offers a free tier for automated browser and mobile app testing, suitable for small projects and open source CI/CD workflows.

entry.title: Testspace.com
entry.description: Testspace.com provides cloud-based test result management and visualization for software teams.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier for public/open source projects and limited private repos.
caveats: Some integrations may require paid plans

useCases: Aggregating test results from CI pipelines
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Testspace.com account.
Connect your GitHub or GitLab repository.
Integrate Testspace into your CI pipeline using provided instructions.
Push test results to Testspace from your CI jobs.
View and share test dashboards with your team.
entry.whenToUse: Use Testspace.com when you need to aggregate and visualize automated test results from CI pipelines in one place.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced test management features or need support for many private projects without a paid plan.
entry.body: Testspace.com offers a simple way to collect, visualize, and share automated test results, especially for teams using CI/CD workflows.

entry.title: TestTLS.com
entry.description: TestTLS.com provides free online TLS/SSL server testing and analysis tools for websites.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free online TLS/SSL server tests.

useCases: Check TLS/SSL configuration of a website
quickstartSteps: Go to https://testtls.com.
Enter your server domain or IP address in the search box.
Click the 'Test' button to start the scan.
Review the detailed TLS/SSL report generated.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a quick, free check of a public server's TLS/SSL configuration without registration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need automated API access, private/internal server testing, or advanced enterprise reporting.
entry.body: TestTLS.com is a free online tool for analyzing and reporting on the TLS/SSL configuration of public servers, ideal for quick security checks.

entry.title: Visual Debug
entry.description: Visual Debug enables real-time visual bug reporting and feedback directly on your website with screenshots and annotations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows limited bug reports and projects for individuals or small teams.

useCases: Collecting annotated bug reports from users
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Visual Debug account.
Create a new project for your website.
Install the Visual Debug script or browser extension.
Invite team members if needed.
Start reporting bugs directly on your site with screenshots and annotations.
entry.whenToUse: Use Visual Debug when you need quick, visual bug reporting and feedback for websites or web apps, especially during development or QA.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited projects, advanced integrations, or need bug tracking for non-web platforms.
avoidIf: working on mobile or desktop apps
entry.body: Visual Debug provides a simple way to collect visual bug reports and feedback directly on your website, making collaboration between developers, designers, and testers more efficient. The free tier is suitable for individuals or small teams needing basic bug tracking and feedback tools, but has limitations on projects and integrations. It's best for web-based projects in early-stage or light production environments.
